slide_1_Cover: AI-Native Fintech OS for Youth Sports
slide_2_Idea_Problem_Solution_Value: 1.7% GMV take-rate scales with transaction volume — structurally superior to per-seat SaaS pricing model
AI-native from Day 1; payment and ops data compounds AI performance, building defensible data moat over time
KG Inicis + Toss native rails live today; competitors face 12-18mo localization barrier to enter APAC market
KG Inicis/Stripe payment rail unifies all fees and plans into one licensed channel at 1.7% net take-rate
Scheduling, attendance, roster, comms unified on payment rail — replaces Excel, KakaoTalk, Band, Calendar
AI agent auto-collects payments, flags at-risk players, generates reports — eliminates 99% of admin work
50M youth athletes in $500B economy widely runs on chat apps, spreadsheets, and cash — zero purpose-built club infrastructure
Club directors manage 15-30 families manually, spending hours weekly chasing fees instead of coaching players
Parents lack payment and development visibility; clubs have no P&L, financial history, or credit access to grow
slide_3_Market_1_3_TAM_SAM_SOM_+_Why_Now: The global grassroots youth sports economy
BM expansion in 6 priority APAC markets

Global grassroots sports represent $300B–400B in annual economic activity. Assuming full platform ownership across equipment commerce, coaching, events, facilities, and insurance, and applying category-appropriate platform margins (10–30%) = $60B annually
~$1.2b annual revenue opportunity across 6 APAC markets, including the base business model (payments + SaaS) and two additional high-margin adjacencies (e.g. insurance, coaching, camps)

Across ~200K–300K teams Korea, Australia, Japan, Singapore, Indonesia, and Thailand from Scout’s current base model, combining payment take-rate revenue(1.7%), club ops SaaS, and near-term AI coaching premium
Founder’s family embedded in Korean elite youth soccer; KG Inicis + Toss APIs now mature for embedded payments and subscription plans
AI ops agents hit cost threshold 2024-25; Korean education spend grew 60% in 10y ($21B)
Sports and leisure structurally resilient to AI disruption, APAC youth sports growth expected to outpace NA/EU(single digit CAGR)
Club ops(small businesses) pain is universal globally; Stripe Connect enables APAC expansion — Korea→Australia→Japan path

Full-stack club OS: payments + attendance + comms + scheduling in one workflow — competitors solve only 1-2 layers effectively each

slide_10_Investor_Assessment: Large underlying economy(~$300–400B global grassroots sports) + ~$60B theoretical platform TAM
Value capture requires multi-layer expansion, real opportunity dependent on execution up stack
Fragmented landscape with no dominant global platform
Incumbents such as TeamSnap and Spond are regionally constrained and lack APAC access
Early defensibility driven by payment integration and workflow lock-in
AI layer remains unproven and network effects are not yet established
Initial validation with live payments and 3 active teams; 18 warm pipeline across 7 regions
However, conversion rates, retention, and repeat usage remain unproven
Core problem is universal and Stripe-based expansion path is credible
However, localization and fragmented market structure introduce execution complexity across regions
Strong founder-market fit combined with relevant fintech and technical expertise
CPO commitment and distributed setup introduce some execution risk

Large but execution-dependent market with a credible APAC wedge and limited direct competition. Early product validation exists, but traction and defensibility (especially AI) remain unproven. Strong team with clear founder-market fit; success hinges on scaling beyond payments into a broader platform.
